response:
Sec. 1  
In 2019, Andhra Pradesh had a Chief Minister. The Chief Minister was Chandrababu Naidu. He was the leader of the Telugu Desam Party.  

***  
Chandrababu Naidu was born in 1952. He served as the Chief Minister for several years. His leadership was known for economic growth.  

***  
He was elected to the Lok Sabha multiple times. His political career started in the 1980s. He became a prominent leader in the state.  

***  
In 2014, he led the Telugu Desam Party to victory. He formed the government with the support of other parties. His tenure was marked by development projects.  

***  
He focused on infrastructure and education reforms. His policies aimed at improving the state's economy. He also worked on rural development.  

***  
Chandrababu Naidu was re-elected in 2019. His party won a majority in the assembly elections. He continued his governance with new initiatives.  

***  
He faced challenges like economic slowdown and unemployment. His government implemented various welfare schemes. He also addressed corruption issues.  

***  
Chandrababu Naidu's leadership had a lasting impact. His policies shaped the state's future. He remained a key political figure in Andhra Pradesh.
